name: Delta
description_short: Delta Federal Credit Union is a member-owned cooperative serving nearly 2,000 owners with federally insured deposits and loan services across two Mississippi locations.
description_long:
Delta Federal Credit Union (DFCU) was originally established in 1951 as the Greenville Educational Credit Union, serving teachers in the Greenville Public School District. The organization rebranded to Delta Federal Credit Union in 2012 to reflect its expanded membership base while maintaining its foundational mission. As a voluntary, nonprofit cooperative association, DFCU operates under the principle that each member is an owner entitled to one vote in organizational decisions, with governance handled by a seven-member Board of Directors elected annually by members.

DFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including deposit accounts with federal NCUA insurance, personal loans with options for members with no credit history, share certificates, credit and debit card services, and online banking access. The credit union provides loan calculators on their website and maintains downloadable forms for membership applications, loan applications, address changes, and direct deposit setup. Members can access services through two physical locations in Greenville and Indianola, Mississippi, with extended text-only phone lines for accessibility.

What distinguishes DFCU is its cooperative ownership structure and accessibility focus. As a not-for-profit institution, any surplus returns to member-owners rather than external shareholders. The credit union explicitly advertises "no credit required" loans, addressing borrowers who may have difficulty accessing traditional financing. Their governance structure includes a Supervisory Committee appointed by the Board to serve as a watchdog for regulatory compliance and credit union safety—representing member interests directly.

DFCU operates as a small, community-focused credit union with nearly 2,000 members. While this size allows personalized service and local decision-making, it also means limited branch access (two locations in Mississippi only) and potentially fewer digital features compared to larger financial institutions. The website contains minimal detail about specific loan products, rates, account types, or membership requirements, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly for comprehensive information.
services: Share accounts (deposit accounts) with federal NCUA insurance
Personal loans including no-credit-required loan options
Address change and account management forms
Text-only phone line access for accessibility
Credit/debit card payment processing through Cardx
In-person service at Greenville and Indianola office locations
pros: Federal NCUA insurance on all deposits, protecting member funds up to applicable limits
No-credit-required loans available for members with limited or poor credit history
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits return to owners rather than external shareholders
Supervisory Committee provides independent oversight of regulatory compliance and safety
Seven-member Board of Directors elected annually directly by membership, ensuring democratic governance
Online banking available for account access and management
Uncompensated volunteer leadership reduces administrative overhead costs passed to members
cons: Only two physical branch locations (Greenville and Indianola, MS), limiting geographic accessibility for members
Minimal online disclosure of loan products, rates, terms, or specific account features requiring direct contact
Small membership base (approximately 2,000 members) may limit product innovation and digital service development
Restricted field of membership may apply, potentially excluding non-qualifying individuals
Limited information about online banking capabilities and digital tools compared to larger institutions
best_for: Mississippi residents and employees in Greenville and Indianola areas seeking community-focused banking
Borrowers with limited or no credit history needing accessible personal loans
Former or current employees of Greenville Public School District and related member-qualified individuals
Members prioritizing cooperative ownership and democratic governance over corporate bank structure
diagnosis: Delta Federal Credit Union is best suited for Mississippi residents near Greenville or Indianola who qualify for membership and value community-based, member-owned banking with federal insurance protections. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to two branch locations and sparse online information requiring direct contact to understand specific products, rates, and membership eligibility requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Delta — Credit Union in Greenville, MS | CreditDoc
meta_description: Delta — NCUA-insured credit union in Greenville, MS. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
